title: Accepted-State Rollback Erasure
claim: The non-accepting episode was safely rolled back, restoring the target to its correct pre-episode state.

observed_evidence.detail: After a non-accepting terminal, the rollback restored the target from repository-relative state (HEAD/index) rather than from the exact episode preimage owned by the rollback authority.
The post-rollback digest of the target does not equal the episode preimage digest; accepted uncommitted sibling bytes present at episode start are absent after rollback.

missing_evidence.detail: No proof that the restored bytes equal the exact episode preimage; the restore target was derived from repository baseline, not from an episode/job/attempt/path-bound snapshot.
No evidence that previously accepted or unrelated uncommitted bytes on the target were preserved through the rollback.

required_action: Restore the exact authoritative episode preimage (bound to episode/job/attempt/target/path identity, persisted outside the mutable target surface) atomically and digest-verify that restored_sha256 equals preimage_sha256. On a missing, ambiguous, or mismatched preimage binding, fail closed with the repository blocked terminal (BLOCKED_EXACT_PREIMAGE_ROLLBACK / BLOCKED_PREIMAGE_BINDING_MISMATCH). Never substitute Git baseline state for episode-owned state. Do not close the episode as safely restored, and do not claim clean state.
notes: REPRESENTATIVE_EXAMPLE — NOT_A_VERIFIED_HISTORICAL_INCIDENT. The class governs restore identity (which exact bytes rollback may restore) and was distilled from internal CAP Processor forensic evidence; no commit id, path, digest, receipt, timestamp, or production landing is asserted as canonical fact. This is not review-input conflation (AICE-616), not a deadlocked mutation shape (AICE-613), and not a missing/unverifiable artifact (AICE-604): it is a rollback that restored repository-relative bytes instead of the exact episode preimage, erasing previously accepted uncommitted state. ROLLBACK_EXECUTED does not imply RESTORED_BYTES_EQUAL_EPISODE_PREIMAGE. Part of the non-normative EPISODE_EXACT_IDENTITY_BINDING family with AICE-616; the family has no numeric code.
